global.title: Bee Node Infrastructure

coreIdeas.contextPhrase: Minimum-requirements baseline: full node, mutable stamp, wallet, open P2P ports.
Bee v2.7.1+ plus depth-22+ stamp; dev node lacks SOC/feeds — the version+depth constraint.
Chat-specific: both users need full Bee nodes because light nodes cannot receive PSS/GSOC.
GSOC subscriptions require full nodes + mutable stamps — impacts live collab + chat notifications.
Bee v2.7.0 auto-races legacy vs wrapped feed versions — removes a class of adapter bugs.
Feed-backing stamps must be mutable to avoid bucket exhaustion — concrete stamp pattern.
Postage stamp bucket model (65,536 buckets keyed by first 16 bits) — capacity-planning foundation.

global.description: Operational requirements and constraints for running Connect on Swarm: full Bee v2.7+ nodes, mutable depth-22+ stamps, wallet, open P2P ports.
global.orientation: Connect-on-Swarm has a hard floor of operational requirements: Bee v2.7.1+ full nodes (light nodes can't receive PSS or GSOC), mutable depth-22+ postage stamps, an Ethereum wallet, and open P2P ports. Within that, feed-backing stamps must be mutable to avoid bucket exhaustion, GSOC subscriptions only work on full nodes, and Bee v2.7.0 auto-races legacy vs. wrapped feed versions so adapter code no longer picks a version. This MOC is the pragmatic deployment reference for anyone onboarding a Bee node for Connect.
